

He's OK, but I take everything with a grain of salt. He did a video on a study about sweeteners. The study showed that in humans certain ones had a negative impact on the glycemic response, they then causally linked that gut microbiome and implanted the gut microbiome from humans to mice and showed that it impaired the glycemic response in the mice.

He said that it was probably the placebo effect for the change in glycemic response, even though they had controls and other sweeteners that didn't show that effect. Also how does placebo effect explain the mice having an impaired glycemic response when the mice didn't even consume the sweeteners. Then he said that the change in gut microbiome isn't necessarily bad, but that doesn't take into account how it had a negative impact on the glycemic response in the mice.

It seems like he obviously didn't actually read the whole paper. And he shits on others for not reading the actual paper and has the catchphrase about human randomised control trials. But here you have one of the best human randomised control trails possible(even he admitted that), but he ignore it.

So he seems fairly ideologically driven. You can guess his opinion on anything by just thinking what would the reddit hivemind say on topic x.

[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q1unt2LwFwk](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q1unt2LwFwk)

[https://www.cell.com/cell/fulltext/S0092-8674(22)00919-9](https://www.cell.com/cell/fulltext/S0092-8674\(22\)00919-9)

[I Was WRONG About Artificial Sweeteners? | Educational Video | Biolayne](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=q1unt2LwFwk&t=46s)

![](data:image/png;base64,)

YOUtube

t's funny you mention bad faith in terms of not applying things equally both ways. In literally every single video you mention "Human randomised control trials". But in the one high quality human randomised control trial that even you said was good quality, you ignored the fact sweeteners were shown to impair glycemic response. You also make fun of people not reading the study, but you obviously didn't read that study since you said that it might be explained by placebo, but in that study they tranplanted the gut microbiome to mice, there is no way the placebo effect explains the impaired glycemic response in mice that didn't even consume the sweetener. The best good fairth argument is that you never read the study but were relying on notes from someone in your team.

From <[https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xkH0PQSl--s](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=xkH0PQSl--s)>
